LEV1.notes: This matches the user-reported c160 enemies using 2X2 sprites 147-150. It does not yet explain spawn positions/count. It only explains a plausible sprite-frame selection path.

pass60_update.sprite_formula_context: The 0x93..0x96 formula is inside the large behavior-code region, but not yet directly mapped to a clean behavior table entry. It may be reached as a fall-through/sub-state inside a handler, or the disassembly entry point is not exactly aligned to instruction boundaries.

possible_spawn_or_script_system.behavior_0x9D: dispatches to 0x83D2, which uses structures around 0x9682/0x968C/0x9696/0x96A0 and script-call style code
possible_spawn_or_script_system.note: This may be related to formations or scripted objects; it is not yet confirmed as the c160 enemy spawner.

pass67_caveat.finding: The code at 0x8615 computes 0x93+[0x233C], but no direct behavior-table entry or direct jump target to 0x8615 was found in the static table.
pass67_caveat.interpretation: The formula still matches the user-reported sprites, but we should confirm its runtime owner via object-pool dump instead of trusting static control-flow alone.
pass67_caveat.practical_test: When c160 enemies are visible, dump object pools and look for active sprite 147..150. Their +0x18 behavior will identify the actual owner.

related_behavior.0xDE86: sets object +0x18 = 0x93 after lcall, likely transitions pickup/drop object into behavior 0x93
related_behavior.0xDE8C..0xE09E: behavior 0x93 update logic / pickup movement or collection checks

pickup_drop_sprite_formula.why_it_matters: User reported c160 enemy drops 2X2 sprite 71. This formula explains sprite 71 if object +0x06/drop_type is 12.

pass62_update.interpretation: drop_type=12 is probably produced via a table/register path, not a simple immediate mov to object+0x06

new_related_code.0xA947: same sprite formula object+0x08 = object+0x06 + 0x3B appears in a level-specific object/pickup routine
new_related_code.0xA96F..0xA9FC: same object+0x06 base value can be remapped to level-specific sprite ranges depending on current level variable 0x2356

scroll_0xEA0_four_object_table.status: strong pattern but table contents not decoded
scroll_0xEA0_four_object_table.record_format: four records, each record has three words: object+0x02, object+0x04, object+0x08
scroll_0xEA0_four_object_table.notes: This is the first clearly identified code-created 4-object formation. It may not be the c160 formation because the trigger is scroll 0xEA0, but it proves the storage model: formations can be tables of off-grid object x/y/sprite triples in DS memory.

c160_watch_sprites.why: User-reported c160 enemies use sprites 147-150 and drop sprite 71.
c160_watch_sprites.expected_result: Active object slots with sprite 147..150 should reveal exact x/y and behavior.

LEV1_c160_4_enemies.user_feedback: These four enemies do not drop a pickup; they stay briefly and then fly downward.
